title: NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure and Patch Management Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(e) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(e) (Security in acquisition, development and maintenance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.3: NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure and Patch Management Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.3' running on index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln" (cve, severity, plugin_name, host, first_found, last_fixed, state), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=vulnerability` `sourcetype="tenable:vuln"` (cve, severity, plugin_name, host, first_found, last_fixed, state)
spl:
index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active"
| eval host=coalesce(host, hostname, dns_name)
| eval first_found=coalesce(first_found, first_seen)
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_found)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, 1=1,90)
| eval sla_breach=if(age_days>sla_days, 1, 0)
| stats count as open_vulns, max(age_days) as max_age by host, severity
| where sla_breach=1
| sort - max_age
| table host, severity, open_vulns, max_age, sla_days
description: Tracks CVE exposure and remediation latency from first detection to fix to demonstrate systematic vulnerability handling for essential and important entities.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vulnerability and sourcetypes tenable:vuln. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Install Tenable Add-On (4060) and route data to `index=vulnerability`; (2) validate field names (`cve_id` vs `cve`, `first_seen` vs `first_found`) in Data Summary; (3) tune `sla_days` to your security policy; (4) integrate with change/patch tickets for exception tracking. Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tenable:vuln`.
visualization: Table (over-SLA assets), Bar chart (count by severity), Line chart (open critical CVE trend).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Scanner findings that are false positives, accepted risks with a formal exception, or test assets outside the regulatory perimeter.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vulnerability` `sourcetype="tenable:vuln"` (cve, severity, plugin_name, host, first_found, last_fixed, state).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Install Tenable Add-On (4060) and route data to `index=vulnerability`; (2) validate field names (`cve_id` vs `cve`, `first_seen` vs `first_found`) in Data Summary; (3) tune `sla_days` to your security policy; (4) integrate with change/patch tickets for exception tracking.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active"
| eval host=coalesce(host, hostname, dns_name)
| eval first_found=coalesce(first_found, first_seen)
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_found)/86400, 1)
| eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, 1=1,90)
| eval sla_breach=if(age_days>sla_days, 1, 0)
| stats count as open_vulns, max(age_days) as max_age by host, severity
| where sla_breach=1
| sort - max_age
| table host, severity, open_vulns, max_age, sla_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Vulnerability Disclosure and Patch Management Tracking (Art. 21(2)(e))** — Tracks CVE exposure and remediation latency from first detection to fix to demonstrate systematic vulnerability handling for essential and important entities.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vulnerability` `sourcetype="tenable:vuln"` (cve, severity, plugin_name, host, first_found, last_fixed, state). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vulnerability; **sourcetype**: tenable:vuln. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vulnerability, sourcetype="tenable:vuln". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **host** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **first_found** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **host, severity**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Filters the current rows with `where sla_breach=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

See also the **CIM SPL** field in this use case; enable the matching data model acceleration if you run the tstats variant in production.

Output fields: age_days, first_found, host, max_age, open_vulns, severity, sla_breach, sla_days.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the vulnerability scanner console (Tenable, Qualys, or your standard tool) for the same asset and scan window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (over-SLA assets), Bar chart (count by severity), Line chart (open critical CVE trend).
grandmaExplanation: We track CVE exposure and remediation latency from first detection to fix to demonstrate systematic vulnerability handling for essential and important entities.
